tweet.text:
New rule change says private equity can buy NFL teams.
This is good for the fans, good for the players, good for the industry.
Here's why:

The NFL is huge.
Out of the 15 most valuable franchises (any sport) on the planet, 9 are NFL teams.
In 2000, the average NFL team was worth $423 million.
The average team today is worth $5.1 billion.

This means pool of people who can buy these teams has shrunk a lot.
And even for the ones that can, it's a huge strain on the finances.
This means there's not money left over to pay for upgrades for the team (facilities, etc.) and for the fans (stadiums, etc.)

With private equity involvement, we should see that change.
They have the cash to invest more beyond their initial purchase price in upgrading the player's lives and the fan experience.
I'm excited for this!

Side note, players should use this as a way to learn more about investing and private equity.
Going out of my way to learn that stuff early own has paid off for me a lot.

It's that time!!
Good luck this season, @HuskerFootball. #GBR https://t.co/Kt2ZRB4lYD

RT @HuskerFootball: "Lincoln, NE is where I was a boy that turned into a man.
Where I found my home away from home.
Where I discovered th…

Always good to be home.
Thanks for a great weekend, @HuskerFootball. https://t.co/ruKgXEKfrb https://t.co/M6rJQ6BS6W

Future @Huskers? 🏈
My boys had an amazing time touring the Suh Strength and Conditioning Center while back on campus. https://t.co/4wtI0xjkoU
Nebraska's future is in great hands with @RaiolaDylan. https://t.co/OBkoO6m9Lm

RT @HuskerFootball: Chasing 3 - In Season with Nebraska Football - Episode 5
🗓️ Premieres tomorrow at 10:00 AM CDT 📷 Subscribe now: 🔗 http…

💰 Let’s break down what $100,000 looks like in my home state of #Oregon when it comes to taxes.
Spoiler alert: it’s a hefty price to pay!

In Oregon, making $100,000 means you’ll face (approximately):
$14,000 in federal taxes $7,700 for FICA $7,500 in state taxes

Oregonians spend about 23.37% of their income on taxes.
That’s the second highest in the US!
Serious money that could be going towards savings or investments instead.

Oregon is a no sales tax state - which is great for consumers.
But with state income tax rates ranging from 4.75% to 9.9%, it’s clear that our tax system hits hard.
Especially for those earning over $125,000.

Here’s a breakdown for you
Which state should I cover next? https://t.co/JIhBS8eR2t
